title: ampelopsis heterophylla var. delavayana
intro: Ampelopsis heterophylla var. delavayana, also known as Delavay vine, is a type of plant species within the grape family (Vitaceae). It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestion, as well as potentially exhibiting antibacterial and antiviral properties. The herbal preparations of Ampelopsis heterophylla var. delavayana typically involve the leaves and stems being dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
